ui.title: Prayer Teacher
ui.stepsIntro: Click on the cards below to learn the steps of prayer.
ui.recitationsIntro: Prayers and Surahs recited in Salah.
ui.typesIntro: Rakat counts and details of the 5 daily prayers.
ui.invalidatorsIntro: List of things that invalidate prayer.
ui.resultGood: Good! You can study a bit more.

1.name: Niyyah (Intention)
1.description: Make intention in your heart for the prayer you will perform.
1.detail: Intention is made with the heart. Saying it with the tongue is Sunnah. Example: "I intend to perform the Fard of today's Dhuhr prayer for the sake of Allah."

2.name: Takbir al-Ihram
2.description: Raise your hands to your ears and say "Allahu Akbar".
2.detail: Men raise their hands to ear level, women to shoulder level. Thumbs may touch the earlobes.

3.name: Qiyam
3.description: Standing position. Right hand is placed over the left hand.
3.detail: Subhanaka, A'udhu-Basmala, Fatiha and a Surah are recited. Eyes look at the place of Sujood.

4.name: Ruku
4.detail: Hands grasp the knees, back is straight. Say "Subhana Rabbiyal Azim" 3 times. While rising, say "Sami'allahu liman hamidah".

5.name: Qawmah
5.description: Stand up from Ruku saying "Rabbana lakal hamd".
5.detail: Stand completely straight, hands released to sides. Pause for a moment then go down for Sujood.

6.name: Sujood
6.description: Go down to prostration saying "Allahu Akbar".
6.detail: First knees, then hands, then forehead and nose touch the ground. Say "Subhana Rabbiyal A'la" 3 times.

7.name: Jalsah
7.detail: Say "Rabbighfirli". Hands rest on the knees.

8.name: Qa'dah (Sitting)
8.description: Sit in the last rakat and recite At-Tahiyyat.
8.detail: At-Tahiyyat, Salli-Barik and Rabbana prayers are recited. The index finger is raised.

9.name: Salam
9.description: End the prayer by giving salam to the right and left.
9.detail: Say "Assalamu alaykum wa rahmatullah" turning head first to the right, then to the left.

subhaneke.name: Subhanaka
subhaneke.meaning: O Allah! You are pure and far from any deficiency. I praise You. Your name is blessed. Your majesty is exalted. There is no god but You.

fatiha.name: Surah Al-Fatiha
fatiha.meaning: In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful. [All] praise is [due] to Allah, Lord of the worlds. The Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful. Sovereign of the Day of Recompense. It is You we worship and You we ask for help. Guide us to the straight path. The path of those upon whom You have bestowed favor, not of those who have evoked [Your] anger or of those who are astray.

ihlas.name: Surah Al-Ikhlas
ihlas.meaning: Say, "He is Allah, [who is] One. Allah, the Eternal Refuge. He neither begets nor is born. Nor is there to Him any equivalent."

ruku.name: Ruku Tasbih
ruku.meaning: Glory be to my Lord, the Almighty.

secde.name: Sujood Tasbih
secde.meaning: Glory be to my Lord, the Most High.

ettehiyyatu.name: At-Tahiyyat
ettehiyyatu.meaning: All compliments, prayers and pure words are due to Allah. Peace be upon you, O Prophet, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings. Peace be upon us and on the righteous slaves of Allah. I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, and I bear witness that Muhammad is His slave and His Messenger.

salli.name: Allahumma Salli
salli.meaning: O Allah, send prayers upon Muhammad and upon the family of Muhammad, as You sent prayers upon Ibrahim and upon the family of Ibrahim; You are indeed Worthy of Praise, Full of Glory.

barik.name: Allahumma Barik
barik.meaning: O Allah, send blessings upon Muhammad and upon the family of Muhammad, as You sent blessings upon Ibrahim and upon the family of Ibrahim; You are indeed Worthy of Praise, Full of Glory.

rabbena.name: Rabbana Prayers
rabbena.meaning: Our Lord, give us in this world [that which is] good and in the Hereafter [that which is] good and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.

sabah.name: Fajr Prayer
sabah.note: The Sunnah of Fajr prayer is very important, the Prophet never abandoned it.

ogle.name: Dhuhr Prayer
ogle.note: In the first Sunnah, one sits separately in every two rakats.

ikindi.name: Asr Prayer
ikindi.note: The Sunnah of Asr prayer is performed before Fard and is not an emphasized Sunnah.

aksam.name: Maghrib Prayer
aksam.note: The Fard of Maghrib prayer is three rakats and is the only Fard prayer with an odd number of rakats.

yatsi.name: Isha Prayer
yatsi.note: Witr prayer is Wajib and is performed after Isha prayer.

farz.name: Fard
farz.description: Acts of worship that Allah has strictly commanded. Abandoning them is a major sin.

vacip.name: Wajib
vacip.description: Acts of worship that are necessary to perform, although not established with as strong evidence as Fard.

sunnet.name: Sunnah
sunnet.description: Acts of worship that the Prophet (pbuh) performed and wanted us to perform.

mustehap.name: Mustahabb (Mandub)
mustehap.description: Deeds that earn reward if done, but are not sinful if abandoned.

mubah.name: Mubah
mubah.description: Behaviors that are free to do or not do, carrying no religious ruling.
